title: Route TLS Expiry Detection
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.3.20 operator-managed internal PKI, UC-3.2.18 ingress upstream 502–504 analytics, UC-3.2.13 generic Kubernetes certificate inventory, UC-3.2.42 kubelet renewal health, or service mesh workload mTLS programs. Does not prove private key compromise or CDN-pinned certificate posture outside the cluster boundary.
evidence: Saved search openshift_uc_3_3_8_route_tls_expiry; versioned openshift_route_blastradius.csv in git; weekly CSV exports to restricted openshift evidence index; dashboard drilldowns on openshift:route, certmanager:metrics, and openshift:routerlogs; alert stanza with platform routing keys.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab OpenShift cluster, ingest openshift:route events for an edge Route whose parsed notAfter is within five days while openshift_route_blastradius.csv lists the hostname as production-critical; run openshift_uc_3_3_8_route_tls_expiry and expect severity page or warn with non-null recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After renewing the backing Secret and confirming oc get route shows updated generation with openssl s_client presenting a leaf beyond ninety days, confirm severities return to inventory or info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only.

dataSources: index=openshift sourcetype=openshift:route for oc get route -A -o json snapshots with spec.host, spec.tls.termination, inline PEM metadata, Secret references, and cert-manager annotations; sourcetype=certmanager:metrics for cert-manager controller gauges including certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds when OpenShift Monitoring or user-workload monitoring scrapes the controller Service; sourcetype=openshift:routerlogs for HAProxy access logs with HTTP Host headers used to compute weekly_client_hits and blast_radius_score; optionally sourcetype=openshift:event for Route or Secret sync warnings; sourcetype=prometheus:metrics may carry the same cert-manager lines when not split into certmanager:metrics.
app: Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes (`Splunk_TA_kubernetes`), cert-manager, `oc get route -A -o json` scripted input, OpenShift Monitoring Prometheus (`certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds`)
spl:
`comment("UC-3.3.8 OpenShift Route edge TLS expiry — openshift:route + certmanager:metrics + openshift:routerlogs blast radius. Join openshift_route_blastradius.csv; coalesce notAfter paths; severities page<=7d warn<=30d info<=90d inventory; router earliest=-30d@d")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:route" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval route_namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, metadata_name, object_name, route_name, ""))))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, spec_host, route_host, route_hostname, status_ingress_host, ""))))
      | eval tls_termination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(route_tls_termination, tls_termination, spec_tls_termination, termination, reencrypt_type, ""))))
      | eval backing_secret=trim(toString(coalesce(tls_secret_name, secretName, spec_tls_secret_name, secret_name, key_secret, "")))
      | eval router_shard=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(ingress_controller_name, router_shard, ingresscontroller, "router-default"))))
      | eval na_str=trim(toString(coalesce(notAfter, not_after, cert_not_after, tls_not_after, "")))
      | eval epoch_raw=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expiry_epoch, notAfter_epoch, not_after_epoch, cert_expiry_epoch, "")),10)
      | eval prom_like=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(_value, metric_value, prom_value, "")),10)
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(
          if(epoch_raw>1000000000, epoch_raw, null()),
          if(prom_like>1000000000, prom_like, null()),
          if(len(na_str)>0 AND match(na_str,"^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T"), strptime(na_str, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), null()),
          if(len(na_str)>0 AND match(na_str,"^[A-Za-z]{3}\\s+[0-9]{1,2}\\s"), strptime(na_str, "%b %d %H:%M:%S %Y GMT"), null()),
          if(len(na_str)>0, strptime(na_str, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null()) )
      | eval cert_subject_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(cn, common_name, subject_cn, tls_cn, cert_cn, "")))
      | eval cert_issuer=trim(toString(coalesce(issuer, tls_issuer, issuer_dn, cert_issuer_x509, "")))
      | eval ann=lower(toString(coalesce(annotations, annotations_json, metadata_annotations, "")))
      | eval certmanager_managed=if(match(ann,"cert-manager\\.io") OR match(ann,"certmanager\\.k8s\\.io"),"true","false")
      | eval cm_join=cluster."#".route_namespace."#".lower(trim(coalesce(backing_secret, route_name, "")))
      | eval lane="route_snapshot"
      | fields _time cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join lane ]
    [ search index=openshift (sourcetype="certmanager:metrics" OR sourcetype="prometheus:metrics") earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | where match(_raw,"certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?im)certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds[^\\n]+\\s+(?<pm_value>[0-9\\.eE+-]+)"
      | rex field=_raw "(?im)namespace\\?=\\\"(?<cm_ns>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw "(?im)name\\?=\\\"(?<cm_name>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | eval route_namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cm_ns, namespace, ""))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cm_name, name, ""))))
      | eval hostname=""
      | eval tls_termination="certmanager_metric"
      | eval backing_secret=coalesce(cm_ns,"-")."/".coalesce(cm_name,"cm")
      | eval router_shard="prometheus"
      | eval pm_epoch=tonumber(pm_value,10)
      | eval not_after_epoch=if(pm_epoch>1000000000, pm_epoch, null())
      | eval cert_subject_cn="cm:".coalesce(cm_name,"")
      | eval cert_issuer="cert-manager-controller"
      | eval certmanager_managed="true"
      | eval cm_join=cluster."#".lower(trim(coalesce(cm_ns,"")))."#".lower(trim(coalesce(cm_name,""))))
      | eval lane="cm_prom"
      | fields _time cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join lane ]
    [ search index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:routerlogs" earliest=-30d@d latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(http_host, Host, httphost, req_host, request_host, hdr_host, haproxy_host, ""))))
      | where len(hostname)>3 AND NOT match(hostname,"^[0-9\\.]+$")
      | eval clientHits=1
      | stats sum(clientHits) AS weekly_client_hits BY cluster hostname
      | eval lane="blast_agg"
      | eval route_namespace=""
      | eval route_name=""
      | eval tls_termination=""
      | eval backing_secret=""
      | eval router_shard=""
      | eval not_after_epoch=null()
      | eval cert_subject_cn=""
      | eval cert_issuer=""
      | eval certmanager_managed=""
      | eval cm_join="_blast_".cluster."#".hostname
      | fields _time cluster hostname weekly_client_hits lane route_namespace route_name tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join ]
    [ search index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:secret" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval route_namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, secret_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval backing_secret=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, secret_name, metadata_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval secret_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(type, secret_type, ""))))
      | where match(secret_type,"tls|kubernetes.io/tls")
      | stats max(_time) AS last_secret_seen, count AS tls_secret_seen_count BY cluster route_namespace backing_secret
      | eval lane="route_secret_inventory"
      | eval hostname=""
      | eval tls_termination=""
      | eval router_shard=""
      | eval not_after_epoch=null()
      | eval cert_subject_cn=""
      | eval cert_issuer=""
      | eval certmanager_managed=""
      | eval weekly_client_hits=0
      | eval route_name=""
      | eval _time=last_secret_seen
      | eval cm_join="_secret_".cluster."#".route_namespace."/".backing_secret
      | fields _time cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join lane ]
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane="blast_agg", weekly_client_hits, null()))) AS weekly_client_hits BY cluster hostname
| fillnull value=0 weekly_client_hits
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane="cm_prom", not_after_epoch, null()))) AS cm_exp_epoch BY cm_join
| where lane="route_snapshot"
| eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch, cm_exp_epoch)
| eval days_until_expiry=if(isnotnull(not_after_epoch) AND not_after_epoch>0, round((not_after_epoch - now()) / 86400, 2), null())
| streamstats window=50000 current=t global=f count AS route_seq BY cluster hostname
| eventstats perc90(weekly_client_hits) AS cluster_hit_p90 BY cluster
| eventstats perc90(weekly_client_hits) AS shard_hit_p90 BY cluster router_shard
| eval blast_radius_score=if(cluster_hit_p90>0, round(min(100, (weekly_client_hits / cluster_hit_p90) * 100), 2), 0)
| join type=left max=0 hostname [
    | inputlookup openshift_route_blastradius.csv
    | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, route_host, vhost, ""))))
    | eval lu_criticality=trim(toString(coalesce(criticality, route_criticality, tier, "")))
    | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, app_team, "")))
    | fields hostname lu_criticality owner_team ]
| fillnull value="" lu_criticality owner_team
| eval owner_team=if(len(owner_team)>0, owner_team, "platform-edge-tls")
| eval severity=case(
    match(tls_termination,"passthrough") AND isnull(days_until_expiry), "inventory",
    isnull(days_until_expiry), "inventory",
    days_until_expiry<=7 AND (blast_radius_score>=75 OR match(lower(lu_criticality),"critical|mission|tier0")), "page",
    days_until_expiry<=7, "page",
    days_until_expiry<=30, "warn",
    days_until_expiry<=90, "info",
    true(), "inventory")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page" AND certmanager_managed=="true", "cmctl_status_certificate_then_verify_issuer_clusterissuer_ready",
    severity=="page" AND match(tls_termination,"reencrypt"), "rotate_edge_cert_and_destination_ca_bundle_for_reencrypt",
    severity=="page", "rotate_route_tls_or_custom_ingresscontroller_default_secret",
    severity=="warn", "schedule_rotation_validate_full_chain_intermediates",
    severity=="info", "calendar_track_confirm_external_secrets_or_gitops_sync",
    true(), "refresh_route_export_and_openshift_route_blastradius_lookup")
| where isnotnull(cluster) AND len(cluster)>1 AND len(hostname)>1
| table cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination cert_subject_cn cert_issuer days_until_expiry certmanager_managed backing_secret weekly_client_hits blast_radius_score owner_team severity recommended_action
description: Detects imminent expiry and governance drift on OpenShift Route edge TLS material that HAProxy presents to external clients: edge, re-encrypt, and passthrough termination classes; inline versus Secret-backed certificates; cert-manager Prometheus horizons; custom IngressController defaults on sharded routers; destination CA bundles for re-encrypt; blast-radius ranking from router access logs; and lookup-enriched ownership. UC-3.3.20 covers internal operator PKI; UC-3.2.18 covers ingress upstream 5xx; UC-3.2.13 covers generic Kubernetes PKI inventory.
value: Customer-facing TLS outages become predictable when platform teams fuse API truth, cert-manager metrics, and traffic-weighted hostname importance: one wildcard renewal fixes dozens of Routes but only if Splunk highlights the shared Secret, and one shard-specific default certificate spares confusion when application Routes look healthy. Splunk preserves evidence for auditors that edge trust is continuously inventoried—not openssl spot checks during incidents—while recommended_action verbs shrink mean time to route by naming cmctl checks, chain validation, and IngressController rotation paths.
implementation: Land openshift:route, certmanager:metrics or prometheus:metrics with cert-manager gauges, and openshift:routerlogs in index=openshift; publish openshift_route_blastradius.csv in git; save openshift_uc_3_3_8_route_tls_expiry hourly with multisearch arms earliest=-7d@d for Routes and metrics plus earliest=-30d@d for router logs; route page and warn to platform edge on-call; archive weekly CSV exports with lookup commit hashes for TLS governance reviews.
visualization: Severity-colored table by cluster, route_namespace, route_name, and hostname with drilldowns to openshift:route JSON, certmanager metric lines, and openshift:routerlogs; single-value tiles for count of page severities; timeline of days_until_expiry for top blast_radius_score hostnames.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Web.url) AS web_url latest(Web.status) AS web_status FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Web.src
| rename Web.src AS edge_src
| join type=left max=0 edge_src [
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Application_State.dest
| rename Application_State.dest AS edge_src ]
| where like(lower(app_info), "%openshift%") OR like(lower(web_url), "%apps%")
| table edge_src web_url web_status app_state app_info
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Web summaries on normalized router access logs mapped with url, status, and src, and Application_State for OpenShift ingress entities tagged in dest or info fields; retain ninety days for quarterly TLS attestations paired with openshift_route_blastradius.csv versions.
knownFalsePositives: cert-manager can renew a Secret minutes before Route admission reflects the new resourceVersion; Splunk may still read the previous notAfter until the next oc export wave—correlate Certificate Ready=True and router reload timestamps before paging. Manually pasted inline PEM on Routes without Secret rotation can desynchronize from GitOps desired state; require repository commit hashes in tickets when severity fires on edge termination. Passthrough Routes legitimately lack HAProxy leaf metadata; do not interpret null days_until_expiry as collector failure without checking termination class. Wildcard hostnames and shared Secrets inflate blast_radius_score for a single renewal action; dedupe executive summaries by Secret fingerprint to avoid forty duplicate tickets. Sharded routers may cache TLS material briefly after Secret updates; short openssl mismatches during reload windows are not chain omissions. Self-signed lab clusters flagged by issuer heuristics should be suppressed via lookup criticality dev—avoid compliance language on known sandboxes. Partial intermediate chains sometimes validate on RHEL trust stores but fail on mobile clients; pair Splunk issuer fields with s_client before declaring false positives. Stale openshift_route_blastradius.csv rows can mis-route pages after reorganizations; audit owner_team quarterly. Prometheus scrape gaps drop certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds while Routes remain healthy; require Route snapshot notAfter or cmctl before muting metrics entirely. External Secrets lag can leave a renewed vault record invisible to the cluster; distinguish vault truth from kube Secret truth when incidents overlap. DNS gray traffic to retired clusters can inflate weekly_client_hits without active Routes; validate cluster labels on router logs before blaming current namespaces.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode analytics on edge TLS degradation by cluster and hostname entities

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with the OpenShift ingress and platform networking team, the identity and PKI engineers who govern corporate trust anchors, and the observability staff who operate Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens plus OpenTelemetry Collector DaemonSets that federate user-workload and platform Prometheus scrapes. This use case isolates customer-visible edge TLS for Red Hat OpenShift Routes: the certificates HAProxy-based Ingress Controllers present for application hostnames, including edge termination with inline spec.tls.certificate material, edge termination backed by Secrets referenced from the Route, re-encrypt Routes that add destinationCACertificate trust to upstream pods, passthrough Routes where the pod serves the leaf clients see, sharded IngressController deployments that each may carry distinct default certificate Secrets, and cert-manager Certificate objects whose DNS names align to Route hostnames. UC-3.3.20 remains the operator-managed internal PKI ledger for kube-apiserver, etcd, service-ca, monitoring clients, and other control-plane bundles. UC-3.2.18 remains ingress upstream 502–504 backend health on the HTTP data plane. UC-3.2.13 remains vanilla Kubernetes fleet PKI inventory across kubeadm paths and generic Secret snapshots. UC-3.2.42 remains kubelet CSR renewal health. Splunk becomes the reconciliation narrative that ties periodic Route JSON exports, cert-manager Prometheus gauges such as certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds scraped through OpenShift Monitoring, HAProxy access-style router logs for hostname traffic weighting, and governance lookups into one analyst row per cluster, namespace, Route, and hostname.

Index and permission design land before parsers. Designate index=openshift for openshift:route structured events emitted by oc get route -A -o json style jobs, certmanager:metrics or prometheus:metrics lines when your collector labels cert-manager ServiceMonitor scrapes distinctly, openshift:routerlogs for HAProxy access logs forwarded from openshift-ingress router Pods, and optional openshift:event for admission or sync warnings that cite TLS objects. Issue distinct HEC tokens per environment with least privilege: platform SRE roles may search all sourcetypes; application teams receive filtered views that exclude PEM bodies if counsel requires minimization. Retention should cover at least two renewal cycles for your public PKI standard, typically ninety to one hundred eighty days hot, so post-incident reviewers can replay rotations without cold-store latency.

RBAC and safety: the exporter must use a dedicated ServiceAccount with get/list on routes.route.openshift.io across namespaces the control governs, not cluster-admin on employee laptops. Legal review should confirm Splunk never stores tls.key entries from Secrets; only Route metadata, parsed leaf notAfter equivalents, subjects, issuers, and stable fingerprints belong in indexes. When you mirror inline PEM from spec.tls.certificate, strip private key material at the collector.

Publish openshift_route_blastradius.csv under version control with columns hostname, criticality, owner_team, optional notes for wildcard coverage or retired clusters, and optional expected_issuer_substring for compliance spot checks. Every production hostname that appears on a customer-facing Route should map to an accountable owner_team so paging routes to the correct squad in the first minutes of a horizon breach.

Risk briefing: wildcard certificates create fleet-wide blast radius when a single leaf expires; sharded routers can serve different default certificates; cert-manager renewal lag may leave Prometheus horizons healthy while Route objects still reference an older Secret until reconciliation catches up; partial intermediate chains often pass lab openssl checks yet fail corporate trust stores on first connect; passthrough Routes intentionally hide edge notAfter from HAProxy because TLS terminates inside Pods, so you must track backing Secrets or workload certs separately and treat low Splunk visibility as expected rather than collector failure.

Differentiation recap: user-facing Route and IngressController edge trust, not internal operator PKI, not generic Kubernetes PKI inventory alone, and not upstream HTTP 5xx symptom analytics.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up three complementary lanes Splunk multisearch can fuse: Route API snapshots, cert-manager expiration metrics, and HAProxy access logs for blast-radius ranking.

Route snapshot lane: schedule a Python or Go utility every fifteen to thirty minutes from a management host with kubeconfig authentication. The utility should run oc get route -A -o json, iterate items[], parse spec.host, metadata.namespace, metadata.name, spec.tls.termination, spec.tls.key, spec.tls.certificate, spec.tls.destinationCACertificate, spec.tls.insecureEdgeTerminationPolicy, metadata.annotations for cert-manager.io and external-secrets keys, and metadata.labels for ingresscontroller or shard hints when present. For each Route, parse the first PEM leaf in spec.tls.certificate when inline TLS is used, emit notAfter, subject, issuer, and fingerprint without private keys, and set sourcetype=openshift:route with fields cluster, namespace, name, host, tls_termination, backing_secret reference, router_shard hint, and route_tls_termination normalized to edge, passthrough, reencrypt, or edge variants your parsers standardize. When TLS is only in a Secret reference, emit the Secret name and rely on a companion Secret digest job if you must hydrate notAfter; this UC assumes either inline PEM metadata or merged Secret scans land on the same sourcetype with coalesce-friendly field names.

cert-manager metrics lane: install or enable a ServiceMonitor for cert-manager-controller metrics, ensure user-workload or platform monitoring scrapes /metrics, and forward lines into index=openshift with sourcetype=certmanager:metrics when your OpenTelemetry prometheus receiver stamps that sourcetype, or sourcetype=prometheus:metrics when shared with other gauges. Preserve labels that identify namespace, name, and dns_names when exporters include them. Validate certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds appears in the OpenShift monitoring UI for at least one Certificate before Splunk paging goes live.

Router access log lane: enable HAProxy-style access logging on openshift-ingress router Pods per cluster policy, ship lines to Splunk with sourcetype=openshift:routerlogs, and normalize the HTTP Host header into hostname. Sample fields include status codes, bytes, and timing when available; the closing search only requires reliable hostname and cluster labels across a thirty-day window for weekly_client_hits. If your environment aggregates logs per shard, add router_shard or deployment labels on ingestion so eventstats can compute shard_hit_p90 in the SPL.

Optional openshift:event lane: forward Kubernetes events mentioning Route or Secret objects when you want corroboration for failed syncs; keep volume managed to avoid noisy projects.

Example jq excerpt operators can run during design reviews to confirm hostnames before indexing:

```bash
oc get route -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | [.metadata.namespace,.metadata.name,.spec.host,.spec.tls.termination] | @tsv'
```

Example cmctl verification pattern after deployment:

```bash
cmctl status certificate -n apps prod-example-tls
```

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE sufficiently large for JSON Route bodies, TIMESTAMP_FIELDS respected when HEC time is authoritative, FIELDALIAS rules that copy spec.tls.termination into tls_termination when using INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json.

Pre-flight searches operators should run before saving alerts:

```text
index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:route" earliest=-24h latest=now | head 20
index=openshift (sourcetype="certmanager:metrics" OR sourcetype="prometheus:metrics") certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds earliest=-24h latest=now | head 20
index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:routerlogs" earliest=-30d@d latest=now | head 20
```

Zero-result investigations should block production paging until collectors succeed.

IngressController default certificates: extend the same Route exporter or a parallel IngressController snapshot job to record defaultCertificate Secret references on ingresscontroller.operator.openshift.io objects for router-default and sharded instances, landing either openshift:route compatible rows with synthetic hostname labels such as shard-default or a dedicated sourcetype you join manually; document the choice in runbooks so UC-3.3.8 dashboards include platform-wide defaults that are not modeled as Routes.

Annotation and GitOps nuance: Routes managed by Argo CD or Helm often carry last-applied annotations; cert-manager injects certificate-name hints on Secrets and sometimes on Routes. Capture haproxy.router.openshift.io annotations only when they influence operational behavior such as balance algorithms; they do not replace expiry telemetry but help responders recognize non-default routing policies during incidents.

External Secrets Operator: when TLS material is synchronized from a vault into a Secret referenced by a Route, include external-secrets.io labels or annotations on HEC events so certmanager_managed stays false while ownership still routes to security engineering.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as openshift_uc_3_3_8_route_tls_expiry with a one-hour schedule for page-tier clusters and a four-hour schedule for inventory breadth. Dispatch earliest=-7d@d on Route and metrics arms inside multisearch, earliest=-30d@d on openshift:routerlogs for blast-radius, latest=now globally. Throttle duplicate cluster, hostname rows for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates from info to warn or page.

Pipeline narrative for reviewers: multisearch fans Route snapshots, cert-manager Prometheus expiration gauges, and summarized HAProxy hostname hits so a silent metrics outage does not hide a genuinely stale Secret reference on a high-traffic hostname. coalesce() ladders absorb notAfter versus cert_not_after versus Prometheus _value style fields versus expiry_epoch aliases your exporters emit alongside route_tls_termination normalization. cm_join keys correlate cert-manager Certificate names and namespaces to Route backing Secret names when teams follow conventional naming. eventstats merges blast counts and cert-manager horizons onto each Route row before human-readable severity tiers. inputlookup openshift_route_blastradius.csv adds criticality and owner_team without hard-coding every hostname inside SPL. streamstats provides ordered per-host sequence context for diagnostics; eventstats supplies cluster_hit_p90 and shard_hit_p90 percentiles for blast_radius_score scaling. case() maps severities to page inside seven days with amplification when blast_radius_score or lookup criticality indicates mission-facing traffic, warn inside thirty days, info inside ninety days, and inventory beyond ninety days or when passthrough Routes lack edge notAfter in Splunk. recommended_action verbs route to cmctl checks, full-chain openssl validation, IngressController default Secret rotations, and destination CA bundle updates for re-encrypt Routes.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.3.8 OpenShift Route edge TLS expiry — openshift:route + certmanager:metrics + openshift:routerlogs blast radius. Join openshift_route_blastradius.csv; coalesce notAfter paths; severities page<=7d warn<=30d info<=90d inventory; router earliest=-30d@d")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:route" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval route_namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, metadata_name, object_name, route_name, ""))))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, spec_host, route_host, route_hostname, status_ingress_host, ""))))
      | eval tls_termination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(route_tls_termination, tls_termination, spec_tls_termination, termination, reencrypt_type, ""))))
      | eval backing_secret=trim(toString(coalesce(tls_secret_name, secretName, spec_tls_secret_name, secret_name, key_secret, "")))
      | eval router_shard=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(ingress_controller_name, router_shard, ingresscontroller, "router-default"))))
      | eval na_str=trim(toString(coalesce(notAfter, not_after, cert_not_after, tls_not_after, "")))
      | eval epoch_raw=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expiry_epoch, notAfter_epoch, not_after_epoch, cert_expiry_epoch, "")),10)
      | eval prom_like=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(_value, metric_value, prom_value, "")),10)
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(
          if(epoch_raw>1000000000, epoch_raw, null()),
          if(prom_like>1000000000, prom_like, null()),
          if(len(na_str)>0 AND match(na_str,"^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T"), strptime(na_str, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), null()),
          if(len(na_str)>0 AND match(na_str,"^[A-Za-z]{3}\\s+[0-9]{1,2}\\s"), strptime(na_str, "%b %d %H:%M:%S %Y GMT"), null()),
          if(len(na_str)>0, strptime(na_str, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null()) )
      | eval cert_subject_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(cn, common_name, subject_cn, tls_cn, cert_cn, "")))
      | eval cert_issuer=trim(toString(coalesce(issuer, tls_issuer, issuer_dn, cert_issuer_x509, "")))
      | eval ann=lower(toString(coalesce(annotations, annotations_json, metadata_annotations, "")))
      | eval certmanager_managed=if(match(ann,"cert-manager\\.io") OR match(ann,"certmanager\\.k8s\\.io"),"true","false")
      | eval cm_join=strcat(cluster,"#",route_namespace,"#",lower(trim(coalesce(backing_secret, route_name, ""))))
      | eval lane="route_snapshot"
      | fields _time cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join lane ]
    [ search index=openshift (sourcetype="certmanager:metrics" OR sourcetype="prometheus:metrics") earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | where match(_raw,"certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?im)certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds[^\\n]+\\s+(?<pm_value>[0-9\\.eE+-]+)"
      | rex field=_raw "(?im)namespace\\?=\\\"(?<cm_ns>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | rex field=_raw "(?im)name\\?=\\\"(?<cm_name>[^\\\"]+)\\\""
      | eval route_namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cm_ns, namespace, ""))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cm_name, name, ""))))
      | eval hostname=""
      | eval tls_termination="certmanager_metric"
      | eval backing_secret=strcat(coalesce(cm_ns,"-"),"/",coalesce(cm_name,"cm"))
      | eval router_shard="prometheus"
      | eval pm_epoch=tonumber(pm_value,10)
      | eval not_after_epoch=if(pm_epoch>1000000000, pm_epoch, null())
      | eval cert_subject_cn=strcat("cm:",coalesce(cm_name,""))
      | eval cert_issuer="cert-manager-controller"
      | eval certmanager_managed="true"
      | eval cm_join=strcat(cluster,"#",lower(trim(coalesce(cm_ns,""))),"#",lower(trim(coalesce(cm_name,"")))))
      | eval lane="cm_prom"
      | fields _time cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join lane ]
    [ search index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:routerlogs" earliest=-30d@d latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(http_host, Host, httphost, req_host, request_host, hdr_host, haproxy_host, ""))))
      | where len(hostname)>3 AND NOT match(hostname,"^[0-9\\.]+$")
      | eval clientHits=1
      | stats sum(clientHits) AS weekly_client_hits BY cluster hostname
      | eval lane="blast_agg"
      | eval route_namespace=""
      | eval route_name=""
      | eval tls_termination=""
      | eval backing_secret=""
      | eval router_shard=""
      | eval not_after_epoch=null()
      | eval cert_subject_cn=""
      | eval cert_issuer=""
      | eval certmanager_managed=""
      | eval cm_join=strcat("_blast_",cluster,"#",hostname)
      | fields _time cluster hostname weekly_client_hits lane route_namespace route_name tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join ]
    [ search index=openshift sourcetype="openshift:secret" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval route_namespace=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, secret_namespace, metadata_namespace, ""))))
      | eval backing_secret=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, secret_name, metadata_name, object_name, ""))))
      | eval secret_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(type, secret_type, ""))))
      | where match(secret_type,"tls|kubernetes.io/tls")
      | stats max(_time) AS last_secret_seen, count AS tls_secret_seen_count BY cluster route_namespace backing_secret
      | eval lane="route_secret_inventory"
      | eval hostname=""
      | eval tls_termination=""
      | eval router_shard=""
      | eval not_after_epoch=null()
      | eval cert_subject_cn=""
      | eval cert_issuer=""
      | eval certmanager_managed=""
      | eval weekly_client_hits=0
      | eval route_name=""
      | eval _time=last_secret_seen
      | eval cm_join=strcat("_secret_",cluster,"#",route_namespace,"/",backing_secret)
      | fields _time cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination backing_secret router_shard not_after_epoch cert_subject_cn cert_issuer certmanager_managed cm_join lane ]
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane="blast_agg", weekly_client_hits, null()))) AS weekly_client_hits BY cluster hostname
| fillnull value=0 weekly_client_hits
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane="cm_prom", not_after_epoch, null()))) AS cm_exp_epoch BY cm_join
| where lane="route_snapshot"
| eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch, cm_exp_epoch)
| eval days_until_expiry=if(isnotnull(not_after_epoch) AND not_after_epoch>0, round((not_after_epoch - now()) / 86400, 2), null())
| streamstats window=50000 current=t global=f count AS route_seq BY cluster hostname
| eventstats perc90(weekly_client_hits) AS cluster_hit_p90 BY cluster
| eventstats perc90(weekly_client_hits) AS shard_hit_p90 BY cluster router_shard
| eval blast_radius_score=if(cluster_hit_p90>0, round(min(100, (weekly_client_hits / cluster_hit_p90) * 100), 2), 0)
| join type=left max=0 hostname [
    | inputlookup openshift_route_blastradius.csv
    | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, route_host, vhost, ""))))
    | eval lu_criticality=trim(toString(coalesce(criticality, route_criticality, tier, "")))
    | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, app_team, "")))
    | fields hostname lu_criticality owner_team ]
| fillnull value="" lu_criticality owner_team
| eval owner_team=if(len(owner_team)>0, owner_team, "platform-edge-tls")
| eval severity=case(
    match(tls_termination,"passthrough") AND isnull(days_until_expiry), "inventory",
    isnull(days_until_expiry), "inventory",
    days_until_expiry<=7 AND (blast_radius_score>=75 OR match(lower(lu_criticality),"critical|mission|tier0")), "page",
    days_until_expiry<=7, "page",
    days_until_expiry<=30, "warn",
    days_until_expiry<=90, "info",
    true(), "inventory")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page" AND certmanager_managed=="true", "cmctl_status_certificate_then_verify_issuer_clusterissuer_ready",
    severity=="page" AND match(tls_termination,"reencrypt"), "rotate_edge_cert_and_destination_ca_bundle_for_reencrypt",
    severity=="page", "rotate_route_tls_or_custom_ingresscontroller_default_secret",
    severity=="warn", "schedule_rotation_validate_full_chain_intermediates",
    severity=="info", "calendar_track_confirm_external_secrets_or_gitops_sync",
    true(), "refresh_route_export_and_openshift_route_blastradius_lookup")
| where isnotnull(cluster) AND len(cluster)>1 AND len(hostname)>1
| table cluster route_namespace route_name hostname tls_termination cert_subject_cn cert_issuer days_until_expiry certmanager_managed backing_secret weekly_client_hits blast_radius_score owner_team severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch for platform paging:

```ini
[openshift_uc_3_3_8_route_tls_expiry_alert]
cron_schedule = 0 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = openshift-edge-tls@example.com
action.email.subject = OCP Route TLS $result.severity$ $result.cluster$ $result.hostname$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch openshift_uc_3_3_8_route_tls_expiry | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: materialize openshift:routerlogs weekly rollups into a summary index keyed on cluster and hostname if Job Inspector queue time exceeds internal SLAs; point the blast arm at the summary while keeping raw logs for forensic drilldowns.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth for Route material always starts on-cluster with JSON exports. Run oc get route -A -o json | jq '.items[] | {ns:.metadata.namespace,name:.metadata.name,host:.spec.host,term:.spec.tls.termination}' to compare hostnames and termination types to Splunk rows without pasting private keys into tickets. When Splunk shows a shorter horizon than openssl on the same Secret, first verify indexer time skew, then verify you are comparing the same PEM leaf index after rotations append multiple certs.

cert-manager truth: run cmctl status certificate <cert> -n <ns> for Certificates tied to Route hostnames and compare Ready and Issuance conditions to prometheus gauge timestamps. Splunk certmanager:metrics lines should move in lockstep with Ready=True transitions; if metrics lead the API by hours, widen alert suppression windows for known controller lag.

HAProxy hostname usage: confirm active clients still hit the hostname by tailing openshift-ingress router logs with oc logs -n openshift-ingress deployment/router-default --since=6h | grep <hostname> or the equivalent shard deployment name when routers are sharded. Zero traffic with a nearing expiry may still warrant warn tier for hygiene but should not receive the same executive narrative as a million-hit hostname.

openssl chain inspection: run openssl s_client -connect <hostname>:443 -servername <hostname> -showcerts on administrative jump hosts and compare presented chains to Splunk-parsed issuer and subject columns. Intermediate omissions often appear only on certain clients; document full-chain renewal steps when leaf and issuer fields in Splunk disagree with s_client output.

Synthetic near-expiry: in lab only, create a short-lived edge Route or Secret reference, ingest openshift:route events, and confirm severity becomes page or warn inside the expected horizon bands. Clear the lab artifact after validation.

Negative test: when every Route notAfter exceeds one hundred twenty days and blast-radius lookup marks non-production hostnames, confirm severities fall back to inventory or info tiers and that the alert wrapper returns zero rows for page and warn filters.

Lookup completeness: run a small join that lists hostnames without openshift_route_blastradius.csv coverage and fix CSV gaps before production enforcement.

Publish a dashboard with one row per cluster, namespace, Route, and hostname using the closing table columns, color severities with UI thresholds where page is red, warn is amber, info is blue, and inventory is gray, and add drilldowns to raw openshift:route JSON, certmanager metric lines, openshift:routerlogs samples, and openshift:event text when enabled. Archive weekly CSV exports of the alert table to a restricted evidence index with the git commit hash of openshift_route_blastradius.csv for cryptographic governance packs.

Wire alert actions to platform on-call, optional Splunk ITSI episodes keyed on cluster entities, and ticketing systems with recommended_action, owner_team, blast_radius_score, and direct links to internal runbooks covering edge versus re-encrypt rotations, IngressController default certificate replacement, cert-manager Issuer failures, External Secrets sync drift, and corporate CA enrollment steps. Document ownership: platform edge for router shards and defaults, application squads for namespace-scoped Routes, security for trust-anchor exceptions.

Train responders: Splunk shows timing, traffic weighting, and termination class; remediation follows OpenShift Route documentation and vendor must-gather guidance—never paste private keys into chat.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — edge Route with cert-manager annotations and Certificate renewing inside seven days: expect page or warn severity with certmanager_managed true; validate cmctl status shows issuance progress, confirm router Pods reloaded without manual rollout, and treat transient duplication between Route Secret references and Certificate status as normal until Ready flips false.

Case B — edge Route with manually created Secret and certificate expiring inside seven days on a hostname whose blast_radius_score exceeds ninety: expect page even when cert-manager is absent; open corporate PKI or vault rotation tickets immediately and verify openshift_route_blastradius.csv owner_team receives the page.

Case C — passthrough Route where TLS terminates on the backend Pod: Splunk edge notAfter may remain null; rely on workload certificate inventory or pod-mounted Secrets, keep severity in inventory unless companion telemetry proves pod cert expiry; do not expect HAProxy access logs to reveal handshake certificate details beyond SNI hostname.

Case D — re-encrypt Route with destinationCACertificate nearing expiry: edge leaf may be healthy while upstream trust fails; rotate destination CA bundle and Service serving cert together, validate openssl s_client against the Service cluster DNS name, and escalate if Java trust stores require manual import.

Case E — wildcard certificate covering dozens of Routes: one expiry row may map to many hostnames through shared Secret references; expand blast_radius_score using summed weekly_client_hits per hostname in dashboards and track a single renewal that fixes the entire wildcard cohort.

Case F — sharded router with custom default certificate expiring on one shard only: synthetic Routes may not exist for default hostnames; rely on IngressController exporter rows or synthetic health Routes and compare shard_hit_p90 anomalies between deployments before blaming a single application namespace.

Case G — cert-manager Issuer or ClusterIssuer Failed condition stalling renewal: cmctl and openshift:event surfaces show ACME or DNS solver faults; pair this UC with issuer status metrics, fix challenges, then re-run the saved search after notAfter_epoch updates.

Case H — partial CA chain in inline Route certificate: browsers with cached intermediates look fine while fresh clients warn; compare leaf count in Splunk-derived metadata to openssl s_client chain depth and re-bundle intermediates in Secret or Route spec.

Case I — self-signed or private-CA leaf on an external production hostname: issuer fields and enterprise allow lists diverge; open compliance review, replace with corporate PKI unless the hostname is documented dev-tier in the lookup.

Case J — hostname exceeding one million weekly_client_hits with certificate outside the seven-day SLA: treat as emergency rotation queue, page platform and application jointly, and consider temporary traffic shedding only under change control.

Case K — Route hostname resolving to a retired cluster while DNS still points customers at obsolete infrastructure: severity may stay info with recommended_action refresh_route_export; confirm DNS and load-balancer ownership before deleting Routes.

Case L — healthy control test: wide notAfter horizons beyond ninety days, cert-manager metrics aligned, moderate blast_radius_score, lookup owner_team populated, severities info or inventory only, and alert wrapper silent for page and warn filters—replay quarterly to prove telemetry freshness.
grandmaExplanation: Every public-facing OpenShift hostname has a TLS certificate that the HAProxy router presents at the edge. If that certificate lapses, browsers warn or refuse to connect, and customers cannot reach the application. We watch each route expiry, weight findings by traffic, and warn the correct team early.
hardware: Applies to Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform on bare metal, VMware, AWS, Azure, GCP, and IBM Cloud where route.openshift.io and openshift-ingress router Deployments exist; x86_64 and AArch64 are in scope when telemetry labels remain consistent.
